nodes.name: pair_distance_compatibility_check
nodes.description: Evaluates whether a candidate left/right feature pair satisfies interaction-distance constraints.

inputs.name: L_feature_min_max
inputs.constraints: Expected to encode min/max distance envelope for left feature context.

inputs.name: R_features_distance
inputs.constraints: Distance values for right-side feature candidates.

outputs.name: distance_match
outputs.constraints: True when pair satisfies distance constraints.

nodes.type_signature: (L_feature_min_max: array-like numeric bounds, R_features_distance: array-like numeric, interaction_distance: float) -> bool
nodes.conceptual_summary: Interval-Constrained Pairwise Metric Predicate. This operation performs a feasibility test over pairwise metric relationships by comparing candidate distance values against a bounded admissible interval and a non-negative reference distance. It maps numeric distance descriptors from two entities into a single Boolean decision indicating whether the pair is geometrically compatible under the specified constraints. Conceptually, it is a constraint-satisfaction projection from continuous metric space to a binary validity state.. Applications: Robotics: validating whether two manipulator waypoints can be connected under kinematic separation bounds, Computational biology: checking whether candidate residue pairs satisfy allowable spatial separation ranges in structural modeling, Wireless systems: testing whether transmitter-receiver candidate links meet admissible range constraints for connectivity, 3D computer vision: screening feature correspondences by verifying epipolar or depth-consistent distance envelopes

nodes.name: Weighted Interaction Edge Derivation
nodes.description: Builds candidate binding interactions and computes weighted graph components from feature and distance matrices.

inputs.name: distance_match
inputs.constraints: Compatibility signal from pair distance check.

outputs.name: edges
outputs.constraints: Each edge contains endpoints and associated weight.

nodes.type_signature: (L_features: array-like or sequence, R_features: array-like or sequence, L_distance_matrix: 2D numeric matrix, R_distance_matrix: 2D numeric matrix, interaction_distance: float, distance_match: bool) -> tuple[list of weighted edge tuples, list/set of node identifiers]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Compatibility-Gated Weighted Bipartite Graph Induction. Constructs a weighted bipartite graph between two entity sets by proposing cross-set links and assigning each link a scalar weight from distance-derived consistency signals. Intra-set distance matrices provide local geometric context, while a cross-set distance criterion and a compatibility gate determine whether a candidate link is retained and how strongly it is scored. The output is the induced subgraph defined by retained weighted edges and their participating vertices.. Applications: Robotics and autonomous systems: associate observations from two sensor modalities by constructing compatibility-weighted correspondence graphs., Neuroscience/connectomics: infer cross-region functional correspondences between two node populations using intra-population distance structure., Materials science: link microstructural elements from two characterization spaces via geometry-consistent weighted matching., Cyber-physical monitoring: align event signatures from two distributed subsystems using distance-constrained interaction edges.

nodes.name: resolve_interaction_distance_threshold
nodes.description: Determine the effective interaction-distance tolerance by replacing a missing input threshold with the configured epsilon default.

inputs.name: interaction_distance
inputs.constraints: If None, must be replaced with default epsilon

nodes.name: construct_weighted_interaction_nodes
nodes.description: Create all candidate interaction nodes as the Cartesian product of left/right features, assigning each node a potential-based weight from feature-family abbreviations.

inputs.name: POTENTIAL_FUNCTION
inputs.constraints: Must contain keys for all family abbreviation pairs

nodes.type_signature: (L_features: sequence of left feature objects, R_features: sequence of right feature objects, POTENTIAL_FUNCTION: mapping[(str, str) -> float]) -> list[InteractionNode]

nodes.name: derive_compatible_node_pair_edges
nodes.description: Enumerate unique unordered node pairs, fetch corresponding left min-max and right scalar distances, and retain only pairs whose distances satisfy the interaction-matching criterion.

inputs.name: nodes
inputs.constraints: Each node contains L_feature and R_feature

inputs.name: effective_interaction_distance
inputs.constraints: Used as tolerance in distance matching

nodes.type_signature: (nodes: list[InteractionNode], L_distance_matrix: OrderedTupleDict[MinMaxDistance], R_distance_matrix: OrderedTupleDict[float], effective_interaction_distance: float) -> tuple[set[tuple[InteractionNode, InteractionNode]], set[tuple[InteractionNode, InteractionNode]]]

nodes.name: assemble_weighted_interaction_graph
nodes.description: Build the final weighted NetworkX graph from the compatible edges and full node list.

inputs.name: compatible_edges
inputs.constraints: Edge endpoints must be present in nodes

outputs.name: interaction_graph
outputs.constraints: Weighted graph representation of compatible interactions

nodes.name: networkx_weighted_graph_materialization
nodes.description: Constructs the final weighted NetworkX graph from derived nodes and edges.

inputs.name: edges
inputs.constraints: Must be consistent with node identifiers.

outputs.name: weighted_graph
outputs.constraints: Contains all provided nodes and weighted edges.

nodes.type_signature: (edges: list of weighted edge tuples, nodes: list/set of node identifiers) -> networkx.Graph or networkx.DiGraph
nodes.conceptual_summary: Weighted Relational Topology Materialization. Transforms two finite collections—entity identifiers and weighted pairwise relations—into an explicit adjacency-based topology object. The procedure first materializes the vertex set, then inserts each relation as an edge annotated by a scalar weight, preserving the provided connectivity pattern. It performs structural encoding rather than optimization or inference, yielding a directly queryable weighted graph representation.. Applications: Neuroscience: materializing weighted synaptic connectivity maps between recorded neural units, Infrastructure engineering: constructing weighted utility or transport connectivity networks from link measurements, Computational biology: encoding weighted interaction topologies among molecular entities, Distributed systems: representing weighted communication latency graphs between compute nodes
